channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump says he's not backing down on tariffs, calls them 'medicine' as markets reel
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President Donald Trump said Sunday that he won't back down on his sweeping tariffs on imports from most of the world unless countries even out their trade with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says US not willing to make deal with China unless trade deficit is solved
item.description: President Trump said on Sunday during a gaggle with reporters on Air Force One that he is only willing to make a trade deal with China if the deficit is solved.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Anti-Trump activist boasts of being 'undocumented, unafraid, queer' at rally
item.description: An undocumented migrant activist declared that she was "queer" and "unafraid" of President Donald Trump during an anti-Trump protest in Washington, D.C., on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump economic adviser defends tariffs: Not a 'big effect' on US consumer
item.description: National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett defended President Trump's sweeping global tariffs that have stoked concerns about the United States economy.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Scully, NewsNation

item.title: For these seniors, DOGE's affordable housing pause means suffering longer without AC
item.description: The Trump administration froze funding for a program to upgrade aging low-income housing and make it energy efficient. The move threatens hundreds of projects around the country.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Ludden, MPR News

item.title: Meet the Trump-picked lawmakers giving Speaker Johnson a full House GOP conference
item.description: Newly minted U.S. representatives Jimmy Patronis and Randy Fine spoke with Fox News Digital about their new careers in Congress after they won special elections.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GOP senators back crackdown on foreign students
item.description: Republican senators are brushing off concerns that high-profile arrests and deportations of foreign students may ostracize a group that is a major contributor to the U.S. economy. International students injected $43.8 billion into the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Judge awards $6.6M to whistleblowers who were fired after reporting Texas AG Ken Paxton to FBI
item.description: A judge awarded $6.6 million combined to four whistleblowers who sued Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton on claims he fired them in retaliation for reporting him to the FBI.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Foreign journalists at US-backed media fear being sent to repressive homelands after Trump's cuts
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — After hiding in Thailand for seven years, two Cambodian journalists arrived in the United States last year on work visas, aiming to keep providing people in their Southeast Asian homeland with objective, factual news through Radio Free Asia.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rubio says US is revoking all visas held by South Sudanese passport holders
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Secretary of State Marco Rubio said Saturday that the United States was revoking all visas held by South Sudanese passport holders, accusing the African nation's government of “taking advantage of the United States.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Majority of Americans uneasy over Trump's economic plan: WSJ poll
item.description: Fifty-four percent of voters are against the president’s levies on imported goods while 42 percent of survey respondents said they supported the measure.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Layoffs threaten US firefighter cancer registry, mine research and mask lab
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Government staffing cuts have gutted a small U.S. health agency that aims to protect workers — drawing rebukes from firefighters, coal miners, medical equipment manufacturers and a range of others.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Farmers fear tariffs could cost them one of their biggest markets in China
item.description: Heading into this year, most U.S. farmers were hoping to break even or maybe record a small profit. But now they are faced with losing the biggest export market for many of their crops after China retaliated against President Donald Trump’s tariffs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Here's what happened during Trump's 11th week in office
item.description: President Donald Trump marked the week unveiling an unprecedented wave of tariffs on imports to the U.S., aligning with his position that countries have long taken advantage of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate vote-a-rama on Trump budget bill kicks off
item.description: The Senate on Friday night kicked off its vote-a-rama, a process that is likely to conclude with Republicans adopting the budget resolution that will serve as a blueprint for enacting President Trump's domestic agenda.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth is to travel to Panama for meetings following Trump's demands for the canal
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth will travel to Panama next week to meet its leaders following recent U.S. allegations of Chinese interference in the operations of the critical Panama Canal shipping lane.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court allows Trump administration to cut teacher-training money, for now
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Friday granted the Trump administration’s plea to cut hundreds of millions of dollars in teacher-training money as part of its anti-DEI efforts, while a lawsuit continues.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: TikTok deal pulled after Trump tariff announcement
item.description: A finalized TikTok deal was pulled Thursday after President Trump announced massive new tariffs against China, a source familiar with the negotiations told The Hill. Trump was poised to sign an executive order approving a deal that would have seen TikTok’s U.S.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Interior secretary orders national parks to be open and accessible as workforce is cut
item.description: Under criticism for staff cuts across the country, Interior Secretary Doug Burgum is directing national parks to “remain open and accessible” and says officials will ensure proper staffing to do so.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Politics Friday: Budget begins to take shape as leaders huddle
item.description: MPR News politics editor Brian Bakst talks with the top budget adviser to the governor: Erin Campbell, commissioner of Minnesota Management and Budget. Then, a conversation with the new DFL Party Chair Richard Carlbom.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: China imposes a 34% tariff on imports of all US products starting April 10
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China announced Friday that it will impose a 34% tariff on imports of all U.S. products beginning April 10, part of a flurry of retaliatory measures following U.S. President Donald Trump’s “Liberation Day” slate of double-digit tariffs. The new tariff matches the rate of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Here's how newly imposed tariffs will affect California products
item.description: President Donald Trump’s widespread tariffs could have a profound impact on California’s exports, according to a study from the Public Policy Institute of California.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, Fox 5

item.title: E-Verify legislation starts to move at the statehouse, passing its first committee
item.description: The state legislature is now at the halfway point in the 2025 legislative session and as the end swiftly approaches, lawmakers in Tallahassee are pushing for more immigration reform.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: White House to remove magnolia tree dating to Andrew Jackson
item.description: Trump added that White House staff will preserve the Magnolia tree’s “Historic wood,” which, Trump said, “may be used for other high and noble purposes!!!”
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5
